name: AI Policy Assistants
what: LLMs that draft and red-team legislation.
how: Models find contradictions and unintended consequences.

name: RegTech & Compliance
what: Automated monitoring of regulatory compliance.
how: Rules engines and ML flag violations continuously.

name: Regulatory Tech (RegTech)
what: Technology that enhances the efficiency of regulatory processes.
how: Automating and improving compliance with regulations.

name: Regulatory Sandboxes for Innovation Testing
what: Provides a safe environment to test new technologies.
how: Facilitates the adoption of emerging technologies and their integration into existing frameworks.

name: Regulatory Technology (RegTech)
use: Automating regulatory compliance processes to reduce costs and improve efficiency.
what: RegTech solutions help organizations meet legal requirements by automating document management, reporting, and audit trails.
how: These tools use AI and machine learning to analyze data, detect anomalies, and generate reports in real-time.

name: Regulatory Frameworks for AI Ethics
what: Developing regulatory frameworks to ensure the responsible use of AI.
how: Promoting transparency and accountability in AI development and deployment.

name: Global Resource Management System
use: Creating a global framework for managing resources and ensuring sustainable development.
what: Developing algorithms to optimize resource allocation and distribution across nations.
how: Using big data and AI to model and predict resource needs and availability.

name: AI Governance Frameworks
what: Developing frameworks to regulate and govern AI technologies.
how: Promotes responsible innovation and public trust.

name: Global AI Ethics Frameworks
what: Developed to ensure the responsible and equitable deployment of AI technologies, addressing issues such as bias, transparency, and accountability.
how: These frameworks provide guidelines for ethical AI development and usage, ensuring that AI benefits society equitably.

name: RegTech for Financial Compliance
what: Regulatory technology helps financial institutions meet regulatory requirements and manage risks.
how: This technology automates compliance processes and provides real-time insights into regulatory changes.

name: Regulatory Sandbox for AI
what: A sandbox environment for testing AI technologies ensures both innovation and safety.
how: Provides a controlled setting to test and regulate emerging AI technologies.

name: RegTech for AI Governance
use: Ensuring ethical use of AI technologies
what: Develops frameworks and tools to monitor and regulate the deployment of AI systems.
how: Involves creating standards, guidelines, and legal frameworks to address issues like bias, transparency, and accountability.

name: RegTech Solutions
what: Regulatory technology (RegTech) solutions help businesses comply with complex regulations. They automate compliance processes, reducing costs and improving efficiency.
how: Using AI and machine learning to identify regulatory risks and ensure adherence to legal requirements.

name: RegTech for AI Compliance
what: Develops regulatory frameworks for ethical AI use.
how: Ensures compliance with data privacy and fairness standards.

name: RegTech for AI
what: RegTech tools are used to ensure ethical and compliant use of AI technologies.
how: These systems help organizations comply with regulations and mitigate risks associated with AI deployment.

name: Emerging Tech Policy
use: Governance of emerging technologies like AI and biotech
what: Creating regulatory frameworks to ensure ethical use and development of advanced technologies.
how: Collaborating with international bodies for standardized guidelines.

name: Regulatory Frameworks for AI
use: Ensuring ethical and responsible use of AI technologies
what: Developing policies to govern the deployment and use of AI in various sectors.
how: Collaborating with international bodies to establish global standards.

name: Quadratic Funding
what: A funding formula that weights the number of contributors more than the size of contributions, favouring broadly supported public goods.
how: A matching pool tops up projects using a square-root formula, so many small donations are amplified more than a few large ones; run as recurring funding rounds.

name: Liquid Democracy
what: A voting model where people can vote directly or delegate their vote on a topic to someone they trust, revocably.
how: Delegation software lets a voter pass their weight to a proxy per issue and reclaim it at any time, blending direct and representative democracy.

name: Digital Identity Wallets
what: Government-backed smartphone wallets that let citizens prove identity and share credentials selectively.
how: Credentials are issued to a device wallet and shared with selective disclosure, so a holder can prove one attribute without revealing everything; run as large-scale pilots.
use: Cross-border ID, e-government services, age verification

name: Algorithmic Transparency Registers
what: Public registries where government bodies disclose the automated systems they use to make or support decisions.
how: Agencies file standardised records describing each system's purpose, data and oversight, which are published for citizens and auditors.

name: Citizens' Assembly Platforms
what: Tools that convene randomly selected residents to deliberate on policy and produce recommendations.
how: Software supports sortition (random selection), structured deliberation and collective drafting so a representative mini-public can reach considered conclusions.
use: Deliberative democracy, climate and constitutional questions

name: Regulatory Updates on AI
what: Enacts new laws and guidelines for the ethical use of artificial intelligence.
how: Includes provisions for transparency, accountability, and user consent.
